title: Russia Security Desk
public_claim: A Russia security and history desk that combines contemporary intelligence analysis with historical depth.
distinctive_mechanism: It separates power, briefing, judgment, and governance subrooms so that Russia work can move between actor analysis, source lineage, historical structure, and claim discipline.

flagship_demos.title: Russia source-to-judgment chain
flagship_demos.why_distinctive: The proof must show how source families become bounded analytic variables rather than polished commentary.

fixture.claim: A Russia assessment concerning regime security, military adaptation, or influence operations.

runbook: Choose one visible Russia claim.
Assign source-family roles.
Translate sources into analytic variables.
Run alternative / confidence review.
State public claim boundary and policy-facing implication.
acceptance_criteria: The claim is traceable to a source family.
The variable is explicit enough to be challenged.
Uncertainty is visible in the public wording.

component_demo_cases.claim_to_demonstrate: separates source families and exact-source candidates before strategic judgment; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
component_demo_cases.input_fixture: books, journals, transcripts, maps, lecture notes, local pages, source markers, and visual dossiers
component_demo_cases.operation_under_test: separates source families and exact-source candidates before strategic judgment; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
component_demo_cases.expected_output: admitted Russia source lane as a reviewable artifact
component_demo_cases.evidence_locator: Expected proof artifact: admitted Russia source lane as a reviewable artifact
